column3: The specific point or area on the painting where the sample was taken.
Document Note: Sample Site Model Overview

column1: Document Note: Sample Site Model Overview
column3: This model defines the relationships surrounding the Sample Site within the context of sampling events on paintings following CIDOC CRM and CRMsci principles. The Sample Site (E26) represents the precise point or small area on the painting from which a material sample was taken. It is physically located on the painting or a defined Painting Part (E22) and may also be situated within a broader Area of Interest (E26) allowing multiple related sampling points or observations to be grouped together. Each Sample Site can be linked to digital image regions that visually represent its position and to descriptive or contextual notes incorporated within related documents. The model records both its role in the Sample Taking event (S2) and its former association with the removed Sample (S13). These relationships establish a clear and reusable structure for documenting where samples originate how they are contextualised within the painting and how they relate to analytical and descriptive records across digital and physical domains.
A human-made work of art that forms the physical source object from which the sample is taken.
A physical part removed from the painting for scientific or conservation analysis.
The event in which a material sample was physically removed from the painting.
A textual or digital document recording information related to the sampling process, results, or reasoning.
A defined digital region on an image corresponding to the physical sample site on the painting.
A defined physical component or part of the painting, such as the paint layers, coatings, support, or later addition.
A defined area on the painting used to group related observations, such as a paint passage, damage zone, or specific section.
Various types of PIDs can be used here, please note that again IGSNs are an option.
